faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth problem in my Bayside home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include small holes in wool clothing, cashmere, or upholstery, silken tubes or cases in closets, and small beige or gold-colored moths flying near wardrobes. You may also notice larvae (small caterpillars) or shed skins in affected areas. Our Bayside technicians provide free inspections to confirm infestations and identify the specific moth species.

faqs.question: What factors affect the cost of clothes moth control in Bayside?
faqs.answer: Pricing ranges from $550 to $5,000 depending on the severity of infestation, size of your home, number of treatment areas, and whether follow-up visits are needed. Larger Bayside properties or homes with extensive fabric collections may require more comprehensive treatment plans. We provide detailed quotes after our initial inspection.

faqs.question: Are clothes moth treatments safe for my family and pets in my Bayside apartment?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. We use EPA-approved products and Integrated Pest Management techniques that prioritize safety for children, pets, and adults. Many of our treatments target specific life stages of moths with minimal chemical use. Our technicians will provide specific instructions for any temporary precautions needed during and after treatment.
